background:
The management conducted a visitor survey on opinions about the level of charges for admissions into a nature reserve. Two questions were posed, and the responses were coded for analysis. The survey results from questioning 20 people are summarized in the following tables:

How frequently do you visit the park?

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline Choice given to respondent & Code \\
\hline more than once a week & 6 \\
\hline once a week & 5 \\
\hline once a fortnight & 4 \\
\hline once a month & 3 \\
\hline several times a year & 2 \\
\hline once a year or less & 1 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

What is the maximum you would be prepared to pay to enter this park?

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline Choice given to respondent & Code \\
\hline 50 p or less & 1 \\
\hline up to 75p & 2 \\
\hline up to £1.00 & 3 \\
\hline up to £1.25 & 4 \\
\hline up to £1.50 & 5 \\
\hline up to £1.75 & 6 \\
\hline up to £2.00 & 7 \\
\hline up to £2.50 & 8 \\
\hline £2.50 or more & 9 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

The results from questioning 20 people were as follows:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}
\hline Person & Frequency of visit & Amount prepared to pay & Person & Frequency of visit & Amount prepared to pay \\
\hline A & 3 & 5 & K & 4 & 3 \\
\hline B & 3 & 4 & L & 2 & 7 \\
\hline C & 2 & 9 & M & 1 & 8 \\
\hline D & 4 & 1 & N & 3 & 3 \\
\hline E & 2 & 2 & O & 4 & 6 \\
\hline F & 1 & 3 & P & 2 & 8 \\
\hline G & 6 & 1 & Q & 5 & 1 \\
\hline H & 2 & 9 & R & 3 & 6 \\
\hline I & 3 & 5 & S & 4 & 5 \\
\hline J & 3 & 3 & T & 6 & 2 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

task.query:
Carry out Spearman's rank correlation on the data, and record the following:
$$
r_{\mathrm{s}}:
$$


$n:$
$P$ :
task.answer: $\mathrm{r}_{\mathrm{s}}=-0.482(=-0.533$ if corrected for ties $) \quad n=20 \quad P<0.05(=0.0203$ if corrected for ties $)$

task.query: What do you conclude about the relationship between the frequency of visits and the amount an individual is prepared to pay?
task.answer: There is a significant negative relationship between frequency of visit and amount the visitor is prepared to pay: the more frequent the visits, the less the visitor is prepared to pay.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
